opp.next_step: Confidence 30% 2nd Jul - sent intro email for Jihad and Hakim, to follow up with meeting 10th June - RFP response submitted, some discount retained for future negotiation. 3rd June - Clarifications received - will rebuild the commercials accordingly, there is some complexity around phasing of the business units, but will bundle into one (as we did with SAMI). 25th May - RFP deadline extended to 10th June. 7th May - RFP received, under review - clarifications deadline 15th May, Response deadline 20th May. 30th Mar - Re below - to help him build out budget approval I have built a quick ROI calculator and shared with him a draft output, suggesting we build it out together and co-present to the CFO - may help move it forward and get further up the decision ladder. 25th Mar - Update from CPO... " Thank you for your follow-up and for checking in. Discussions regarding the bid management solution are still underway as we work on securing budget approval , so some activities are on hold for now. I appreciate your patience and will reconnect as soon as we have the green light to move forward." ... moving back to Q2. 13th Mar - Haven't heard anything from CPO since below.... nudged to arrange a catch up after Eid. 23rd Jan - After nudging Jihad the CPO received this update... "I wanted to give you an update on our current progress. We are in the midst of setting our procurement strategy, with a primary focus on the digitalization and automation of our procurement function. At this stage, we have submitted our procurement strategy to the management for their review and approval. Once we receive their approval, we will move forward with the selection process to identify the best procurement solution that aligns with our goals. Let's plan to have our discussion once we have finalized everything internally. I appreciate your understanding and look forward to catching up soon." DQ: Want to try and turn Jihad into a champion - I have offered live demo to the team f2f in Abu Dhabi, also to support the business case and share any helpful case studies & metrics, importantly stressed the need to keep a regular communication cadence ....will look in each time I am in Abu Dhabi to keep the rapport up. Also GMQ 26 communicated. 14th Jan waiting on confirmation of F2F meeting 7th Jan - Pricing submitted to CPO with a request to run through it f2f ASAP. 30th Dec - SaaS received with Hrishi for implementation. 20th Dec - Pricing with Raj for Saas 19th Dec - After several nudges, still no response to the table - spoke to Arun, he said we will have to estimate, can't/won't provide further detail. 20th Nov - Shared a completed/estimated usage table to get pricing - lots of guesses - awaiting confirmation this scenario is realistic. 30th Oct - Met with Jihad (CPO) and Arun onsite in Abu Dhabi, constructive run through the pricing template, but they have a lot of gaps in data for the QIT (including no idea what their revenue or spend is ...not unusual for a ME private co), Arun to come back with some info & DQ to extrapolate into scenarios for a QIT - not as far advanced in the as they had said (can't see how they have shortlisted Ariba and Coupa ... think it's been informal) - vague on timelines est 3-6 months, DQ offered to present to the steering committee and build an ROI/Biz Case if needed.... will keep as end Q4 close and monitor as it progresses. 28th Oct - Confirmed meeting CPO (Jihad) at his office in Abu Dhabi Thurs 30th Oct 22nd Oct - No response yet, suggest a F2F in Abu Dhabi next week 16th Oct - Nudged to arrange the follow up call 8th Oct - Open to reviewing Zycus, but wants pricing, shared the estimation file with him to understand scope, review and arrange a follow up call. 30th Sept - Jihad back from travel - catch up call scheduled for 8th 9th Sept - CPO (Jihad) attended the Abu Dhabi World Tour event - are running a formal evaluation and shortlisted Ariba and Coupa, is interested in adding Zycus, will be travelling, but will reach out to us on his return 22nd Sept... DQ to nudge if no response by 24th.
opp.ais_why:
• On 23 Jan 2026, CPO Jihad wrote to the rep: 'We are in the midst of setting our procurement strategy, with a primary focus on the digitalization and automation of our procurement function. At this stage, we have submitted our procurement strategy to the management for their review and approval.' Buyer framing is 'digitalization and automation' ‚Äî adjacent to AI but not naming AI/Merlin/agentic.
• On 7 May 2026: 'RFP received, under review - clarifications deadline 15th May, Response deadline 20th May.' On 30 Mar 2026: 'to help him build out budget approval I have built a quick ROI calculator and shared with him a draft output, suggesting we build it out together and co-present to the CFO.' Jihad is engaging deeply but the conversation is digitalization/ROI focused. ANA is in product scope (only AI SKU); Merlin/Merlin Intake/Agentic AI are absent.
• Confidence is Medium. National Holding has a real RFP in flight (received 7 May), an engaged CPO (Jihad), and a stated digitalization/automation thesis. But 'digitalization and automation' is process-language, not AI-specific. With only ANA in AI scope and no explicit AI demand from buyer, the score sits at 5 (boundary DOWN per rubric).

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI metrics identified across all evidence. Next Step 2026-01-23 states buyer's goal is 'digitalization and automation of our procurement function' (qualitative pain), but no cost savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or error-rate reduction has been articulated by the buyer. Next Step 2026-03-30 shows Dan built an ROI calculator draft and offered to 'co-build it out together and co-present to the CFO', but no confirmation the CFO presentation occurred or that quantified value was agreed. Next Step 2025-10-30 notes buyer 'have a lot of gaps in data for the QIT (including no idea what their revenue or spend is)', indicating National Holding lacks baseline metrics to anchor an ROI case. Without quantified impact, CFO budget approval remains at risk even if RFP scoring favors Zycus. The highest-leverage move is to complete the ROI co-presentation with Jihad to the CFO, quantifying procurement digitalization value (e.g. X% cost reduction, Y hours saved, Z% maverick-spend elimination) using comparables from similar private Middle East holding companies.

sources: SF Contact Role: Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement, Decision Maker)
champion.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) is the sole engaged stakeholder and a developing champion, but lacks Economic Buyer access and budget authority, limiting his ability to close the deal. Evidence of advocacy: Jihad attended Zycus Abu Dhabi World Tour event 2025-09-09, initiated evaluation of Zycus alongside Ariba and Coupa, hosted on-site meeting in Abu Dhabi 2025-10-30, and engaged consistently through RFP cycle (clarifications, deadline extensions, RFP submission 2026-06-10). Next Step 2025-10-30 shows Jihad opened the door for Dan to 'present to the steering committee and build an ROI/Biz Case if needed', indicating trust and willingness to facilitate internal advocacy. However, Jihad has not introduced Zycus to CFO, management, or steering committee despite Dan's 2026-03-30 offer to co-present ROI to the relevant stakeholder engagement: last contact 2026-07-02 per ground truth LastActivityDate, but this was rep-initiated intro email (awaiting buyer reply per engagement pulse), not a two-way touch. Risk: champion is cooling post-RFP submission (23 days of silence) and may be waiting on internal RFP evaluation or management/CFO approval before re-engaging. The move is to re-activate Jihad with a value-add check-in (share a relevant CPG/holding-company procurement case study or ROI benchmark) and explicitly request introduction to CFO or steering committee to de-risk budget approval gate.

competition.narrative: Competing head-to-head against SAP Ariba (primary) and Coupa in a formal RFP process, per Next Step 2025-09-09: buyer 'running a formal evaluation and shortlisted Ariba and Coupa, is interested in adding Zycus'. Next Step 2025-10-30 notes buyer claim of Ariba/Coupa shortlist appears informal ('can't see how they have shortlisted Ariba and Coupa...think it's been informal'), suggesting National Holding may have had vendor conversations but Zycus entry via the relevant stakeholder event legitimized a structured 3-way RFP. No intel on competitive standing post-RFP submission (23 days elapsed, zero feedback). SAP Ariba is primary competitor given enterprise scale and Middle East presence; Coupa is secondary. Wedge: Zycus differentiation via AI/Merlin intelligent automation (agentic procurement, I2O supplier negotiation) against Ariba's ERP-heavy complexity and Coupa's lighter feature set. Next Step 2026-06-10 mentions 'some discount retained for future negotiation', implying Zycus pricing is competitive but not lowest-cost. Risk: without RFP scoring visibility, Zycus cannot reinforce strengths (AI, ease-of-deployment vs Ariba, procurement-depth vs Coupa) or address gaps before vendor selection. The move is to request RFP debrief call with Jihad to understand Zycus technical and commercial standing, then activate AI/Merlin narrative (ideally via product escalation or executive demo) if RFP response under-positioned Merlin differentiation.

sources: SF Contact Role: Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement, Decision Maker)
identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed pain: National Holding's procurement function lacks digitalization and automation, per Next Step 2026-01-23 buyer statement 'We are in the midst of setting our procurement strategy, with a primary focus on the digitalization and automation of our procurement function.' Next Step 2025-10-30 reveals deeper operational pain: buyer has severe data gaps ('no idea what their revenue or spend is...not unusual for a ME private co'), implying manual, fragmented procurement processes with no spend visibility, category management, or supplier performance tracking. This pain is owned by Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role), who is accountable for procurement transformation. Next Step 2025-09-09 confirms urgency: buyer initiated formal evaluation (shortlisted Ariba and Coupa, added Zycus) in September 2025, indicating executive mandate for procurement modernization. However, pain severity is undermined by budget-approval delays (161 days and counting per Next Step 2026-01-23 to 2026-03-25), suggesting the pain is acknowledged but not yet urgent enough to force CFO budget sign-off. The risk is that operational pain sits with Jihad (mid-level), but CFO sees it as low-priority spend.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, ERP integration) are unaddressed and unknown. Next Step 2025-12-30 mentions 'SaaS received with Hrishi for implementation', suggesting Zycus implementation team (Hrishi, inferred as Zycus resource) is scoped, but no confirmation of buyer-side implementation partner, InfoSec requirements, or legal redlining process. No Current Contract Expiration date in Salesforce fields (not queried or not populated), so no forcing function from incumbent-contract end-date is visible. Next Step 2025-10-30 notes buyer has 'lots of gaps in data', implying immature procurement operations and potentially immature contracting/legal processes, which may extend contracting timeline. With 28 days to close and no MSA/SOW kickoff, InfoSec review, or ERP integration scoping confirmed, the paper process is a critical path risk. The move is to propose a contracting kickoff call with Jihad and National Holding legal/InfoSec teams immediately upon vendor selection to compress the legal/security gates.

sources: SF Contact Role: Jihad Ibrahim (Decision Maker), Chad Bautista (Executive Sponsor, never engaged)
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer (CFO or C-level management with budget authority) is unmapped and has never engaged. Next Step 2026-01-23 and 2026-03-25 confirm 'management' must approve the procurement strategy and budget, and that approval is still pending as of 2026-03-25 ('still underway as we work on securing budget approval'). Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) does not control budget per this evidence. Dan offered ROI co-presentation to the CFO on 2026-03-30 per Next Step, but no confirmation the CFO meeting occurred or that CFO sponsorship was secured. Chad Bautista is listed as Executive Sponsor in contact roles but has never appeared in any Next Step entry, task, or recorded activity, suggesting the role assignment is aspirational or outdated. Without CFO access, budget approval remains a blocking risk even if RFP evaluation favors Zycus. The critical move is for the deal owner's manager (Carl Kimball per ground truth) to arrange executive-to-executive access to National Holding CFO or C-level management to secure budget sponsorship and de-risk the commercial gate.

sources: SF Contact Role: Jihad Ibrahim (Decision Maker), Chad Bautista (Executive Sponsor, never engaged)
decision_process.narrative: Decision process has three confirmed gates: (1) procurement strategy approval by management (submitted 2026-01-23 per Next Step, still pending 2026-03-25), (2) RFP evaluation and vendor shortlisting (RFP response submitted 2026-06-10, awaiting feedback as of 2026-07-03), and (3) CFO/management budget approval (authority confirmed in Next Step 2026-01-23 and 2026-03-25, not yet obtained). Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) is the process owner but does not hold final budget authority. Next Step 2025-10-30 mentions 'steering committee' when Dan offered to present ROI/business case, implying a multi-stakeholder approval body exists, but no steering committee member has been named or engaged. Chad Bautista (Executive Sponsor contact role) has never appeared, raising questions about whether executive sponsorship is real or a CRM placeholder. The call sequence shows only Jihad engagement from 2025-09-09 (Abu Dhabi World Tour event) through 2026-07-02 (intro email), with no executive escalation or multi-stakeholder validation. Risk: even if RFP scoring favors Zycus, the CFO/management gate is opaque and unmanaged.

decision_criteria.narrative: Formal RFP received 2026-05-07 per Next Step indicates structured evaluation criteria exist (technical and commercial requirements per RFP format), but the specific scoring dimensions, weightings, and decision factors are unknown to the relevant stakeholder 2026-06-03 mentions 'some complexity around phasing of the business units' in the RFP clarifications, suggesting multi-entity rollout is a decision criterion. Next Step 2025-10-30 notes buyer 'can't see how they have shortlisted Ariba and Coupa...think it's been informal', implying early-stage criteria were informal and relationship-driven rather than structured. Next Step 2025-09-09 confirms buyer is 'running a formal evaluation' as of September 2025, so criteria shifted from informal to formal RFP-based. No visibility into how Zycus scores vs Ariba vs Coupa on technical fit, pricing, implementation complexity, or AI/Merlin differentiation. The move is to request an RFP debrief or scoring feedback call with Jihad to understand Zycus standing and address any technical or commercial gaps before vendor selection.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 28 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent; Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 26 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 13 is anchored on the stage win (26), nudged by commitment 55 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 28. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
